title: Living in Boulder Vlog 5 - 19 -2017: More Church Fathers

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief life update from Boulder, noting he is deep into reading Church Fathers as part of a project to read all books from the ancient Western world. He discusses how prolific the Church Fathers are and how long the project is taking, and shares excitement about a voice-to-text/text-to-speech app called Voice Reading that he wants to use with Wikipedia articles and potentially for transcribing his videos.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Vastly underestimated how prolific they are; they may have more text than everything else in the ancient world combined
Optimistic he can finish in another week once he powers through the Church Fathers
Very excited about it as a productivity tool for consuming long texts and Wikipedia articles

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been too busy to vlog for a few days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm trying to read all the books from the ancient Western world.

claims.subject: church fathers
claims.claim: The church fathers are very, very prolific.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently reading 'The Pastor of Hermos' by Clement of Rome.

claims.subject: ancient world texts
claims.claim: I'm wondering if the church fathers wrote more text than everything else in the ancient world combined.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I originally planned to finish reading by last Sunday, but it's already the next Saturday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I underestimated how many books there are in history.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm still super fired up and plan to go another week to finish.

claims.subject: church fathers
claims.claim: Once I power through the church fathers, the rest will be easier.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I downloaded a new voice-to-text program for my computer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to see if I can get the voice-to-text to work for writing on paper.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might start putting transcripts of my videos on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Having transcripts would allow me to link to parts of history I talk about.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to Wikipedia articles about Rome using a voice reading program.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a program called Voice Reading that reads Wikipedia articles aloud.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The voice reading program reads very fast, which I like.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The program can read really big pages, like entire books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to try reading an entire book with the voice reading program soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The program stopped when I tried to read a 3600-page text, maybe because I need the Pro version.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm super interested in finding out how many pages of text exist from the ancient world.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to categorize the pages of ancient texts by subject and link them to their original sources.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think linking all the texts to their original pages would be really cool.
